hook: Two licences. One placement. Most composers only know about one.
cta: Save this before your next deal conversation.
caption_full:
Two licences. ↓

If you've ever wondered why sync deals feel complicated — every placement requires two separate legal permissions.

Save this before your next deal conversation.

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #MusicRights #IndependentArtist

slides.body: If you've ever wondered why sync deals feel complicated — every placement requires two separate legal permissions. ↓ "One-stop" means you control both.

🔑 THE SYNC LICENCE
Permission to pair your composition with visual content.
Controlled by: the publisher (or you, if you self-publish).

🔑 THE MASTER LICENCE
Permission to use the specific recording.
Controlled by: whoever owns the master — often the label, unless you recorded independently.

One placement = two separate negotiations. Supervisors prioritise one-stop tracks.
Fewer calls. Faster deals. No surprises.

If a label owns your master:
→ They share in every sync deal you ever make
→ A supervisor has to negotiate with two parties
→ That friction kills deals

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
